model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Duke is the #1 ranked team in both KenPom and Torvik with a 31-2 record, boasting an elite adjusted offensive efficiency (127.9) and the nation's best adjusted defense (88.7-90.4), producing a staggering efficiency margin of roughly +37 to +39 points per 100 possessions. Siena, a 16-seed from the MAAC ranked around 190th nationally with a negative efficiency margin, is outclassed in every measurable dimension. Even accounting for reports of a Cooper Flagg ankle injury sustained during the ACC Tournament, Duke's depth and overall talent advantage make this a near-certain outcome, as 1-seeds have historically defeated 16-seeds at a rate exceeding 99%.

reasoningStep.title: East 1/16: Duke vs Siena
reasoningStep.summary: Duke, the overall #1 seed and top-ranked team in all major predictive systems, faces a massive mismatch against 16-seed Siena from the MAAC. The efficiency gap of nearly 40 points per 100 possessions is among the largest possible in the tournament.
evidence: Duke: KenPom #1 (AdjEM +39.17, AdjO 127.9, AdjD 88.7), Torvik #1 (AdjOE 128.0, AdjDE 90.4), 31-2 record
Siena: KenPom #192 (AdjEM -2.11, AdjO 107.2, AdjD 109.3), Torvik #187, 23-11 record in the MAAC
Cooper Flagg ankle injury reported during ACC Tournament but positive updates suggest he is expected to play

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Cooper Flagg injury update: New details emerge on Duke star ahead of NCAA Tournament - On3 Skip to main content Mobile Menu Teams Teams Fan Sites Forums Shows College College Football News College Football Player Rankings College Football Rankings College Football Playoff College Basketball News Women's Sports NIL NIL News NIL Valuation NIL Deals NIL Deal Tracker Sports Business Transfer Portal Transfer Portal News NCAA Transfer Portal Transfer Portal Rankings Transfer Portal Team Rankings Recruiting Football Recruiting Basketball Recruiting Database Team Rankings Player Rankings Industry Comparison Commitments Recruiting Prediction Machine High School High School News Schools Rankings Scores Draft NFL Draft NFL Draft News Draft By Stars College Draft History College Draft Totals NBA Draft NBA Draft News Pro NFL NASCAR NBA Culture Sports Betting About About On3 App Advertise Press FAQ Contact Join Now Login Duke Football News Roster Schedule Duke Draft History FB Recruiting News Commitments Transfers Team Rankings Player Rankings Industry Comparison Recruiting Prediction Machine Basketball News Roster Schedule Duke Draft History BB Recruiting News Commitments Transfers Team Rankings Player Rankings Industry Comparison Recruiting Prediction Machine Fan Site Search players Search Cooper Flagg injury update: New details emerge on Duke star ahead of NCAA Tournament by: Griffin McVeigh 03/20/25 griffin_mcveigh Read In App Duke enters the NCAA Tournament with major health questions, specifically with star freshman Cooper Flagg . He suffered an ankle injury early in the ACC Tournament but has working toward gearing up for a postseason run with the Blue Devils. One day before their first-round game, positive news has come in. According to ESPN’s David Hale, Flagg is “pain-free” and is set to be fully healthy for Friday’s matchup against Mount St. Mary’s. A massive boost for Duke, getting a National Player of the Year contender back. “Cooper Flagg says he is “pain-free” after suffering an ankle injury that kept him out of the majority of Duke’s ACC tournament championship run, and he expects to be fully healthy for the Blue Devils’ NCAA tournament opener against Mount St. Mary’s on Friday,” Hale said . Flagg suffered the injury facing Georgia Tech Thursday in the ACC Tournament. He wound up missing games against North Carolina and Louisville. Duke was still able to find an immense amount of success in Charlotte, cutting down the nets with another conference title. Mount St. Mary’s may not be the team where Flagg is needed. Duke is a heavy favorite heading into the game after being awarded the second No. 1 seed by the Selection Committee. But a potential second-round matchup against Baylor / Mississippi State would not be easy without Flagg. Getting him back on the court allows Duke to be at full strength. And not many teams have been able to take them down when at that level. Top 10 1 Bryan Hodgson Turns down Syracuse job 2 Curt Cignetti Pace car dri
